summary: Implemented P1-10 semantic RAG progress: added a Hugging Face Transformers feature-extraction embedding provider using Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2, kept local hashing as an honest fallback, generated dense vector proof metadata with semantic_embedding_provider=true, and added local extractive generated-answer quality metrics to RAG eval. Current data now reports RAG proof healthy with 384-dimensional semantic embeddings and eval healthy with 12/12 hybrid cases, 0 lexical fallback, and generated_answer_eval healthy. Overall goal remains partial because fresh Codex Desktop live pre-response proof still requires a user-created fresh thread event.
decisions: Use a real pretrained local embedding model instead of renaming hashing or TF-IDF as semantic retrieval.
Keep local text hashing as an honest fallback with semantic_embedding_provider=false when the model is disabled or unavailable.
Do not commit ignored dense vector index files; they are rebuildable derived artifacts under .dino/index.
Generated-answer evaluation is labeled as a local extractive judge and not presented as full external Ragas/LLM-judge evaluation.
Keep the active goal partial because live Codex Desktop pre-response proof still requires a fresh user-created thread event.
next_steps: Run npm run codex:live-proof, approve the hook if prompted, paste the proof prompt into a fresh Codex Desktop workspace thread, and rerun npm run verify:goal.
For P2-12, make installer/update optionally prewarm or verify the Hugging Face model cache so new-PC RAG proof can rebuild dense vectors without ambiguity.
Consider adding an optional external Ragas/LLM judge path for stricter answer-quality calibration.
